response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with genuine geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS work) and proven low-latency systems work handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic. However, that systems work was in DLang rather than modern C++ or Rust, his hands-on C/C++ experience is dated (2014–2015 at National Instruments), and recent roles center on TypeScript/Node.js product work. He has no direct navigation/routing pipeline experience, so fit for the core C++ routing-tiles responsibilities is uncertain despite strong adjacent skills.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform experience as founder of an open-source Geo-Data platform used in production
Low-latency, high-throughput backend experience (async I/O, caching, fibers) on services handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and demonstrated willingness to learn across the stack
concerns: Modern C++ experience is limited and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No direct experience with routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile production pipelines
Recent work is primarily TypeScript/Node.js product development, distant from systems-level pipeline engineering
